title: Mildly Herbal Vietnamese Sardines Nem Nuong
description: A solo dinner dish featuring sardines, dashi extract, and herbal flavors, this Vietnamese nem nuong is a unique and delicious meal option, halal friendly and easy to make.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine chopped sardines, dashi extract, fish sauce, rice vinegar, grated ginger, chopped scallions, and chopped cilantro, and mix well.
Lay a rice paper wrapper on a flat surface and brush with vegetable oil.
Place about 1 tablespoon of the sardine mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the nem nuong to the skillet and fry until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the nem nuong from the oil and drain on paper towels.
Serve the nem nuong hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
